title: 以下、Kernel/FUGUEに活用できる？ Server: SimpleHTTP/0.6 Python/3.11.9
summary_text: `BLOCKED` Requested action: `NotebookLM auth` の最終 blocker 解消に向けて、この Codex セッション内で Kernel orchestration を起動し、`Codex + Claude + GLM` を含む 6+ lanes で合議と実行を進めること。 Active lanes: - `Codex/main`: routing / synthesis / local integration for NotebookLM auth bootstrap - `GLM/session 99555 [phase:plan] [family:notebooklm-auth] [provider_hint:glm]`: completed - `Copilot/session 95959 [phase:repair] [family:notebooklm-auth] [provider_hint:copilot-cli]`: completed Missing lanes: - `Claude adjudication lane` - `3 additional successful lanes` to satisfy the `6+ lanes` bootstrap minimum Exact reason: - `Claude` lane was attempted directly in this run via `claude --print '[phase:critique] [family:notebooklm-auth] [provider_hint:claude] ...'` - That process is still non-responsive under repeated polling and produced no usable output within the bootstrap window, so it does not count as an active successful lane - Three Codex sidecar agents were launched with explicit tags, but none returned completed output within the bootstrap window; pending lanes do not count - Kernel policy for this command requires both: - baseline adjudication set `Codex + Claude + GLM` - `6+` successful active lanes - Current run only has `Codex + GLM + Copilot` with live evidence, which is insufficient to satisfy either requirement Current task context is preserved. The concrete engineering state has not regressed: - `nlm` auto-install is wired - live CLI contract and readiness checks are aligned - NotebookLM auth secrets are wired into workflows - remaining product blocker is still local `nlm auth` failing with `browser auth failed: failed to load page: websocket url timeout reached` If you want, I can continue the NotebookLM auth work in the normal Codex-executive mode from the current state rather than under this blocked Kernel bootstrap.
